lesson.title: Cramer's Rule & LU Decomposition
lesson.body: Cramer's rule solves Ax = b by xᵢ = det(Aᵢ)/det(A), where Aᵢ is A with the i-th column replaced by b. LU decomposition factors A = LU where L is lower triangular and U is upper triangular. Once computed, it solves Ax = b via forward substitution Ly = b then back substitution Ux = y, which is efficient for multiple right-hand sides.

rooms.question: Cramer's rule gives x₁ = det(A₁)/det(A). What is A₁?
answers: A with its first column replaced by b
A with its first row replaced by b
A with its first column deleted
rooms.explanation: Aᵢ is formed by replacing the i-th column of A with the right-hand side vector b.

rooms.question: In LU decomposition A = LU, which of these is true?
answers: L is lower triangular with 1s on diagonal, U is upper triangular
L is upper triangular, U is lower triangular
Both L and U are diagonal
L has zeros on the diagonal
rooms.explanation: L (Lower) has 1s on the diagonal and zeros above; U (Upper) is the result of Gaussian elimination.

rooms.question: To solve Ax = b using LU decomposition, what two systems must be solved?
rooms.explanation: Split A = LU, substitute: LUx = b becomes Ly = b (forward sub for y), then Ux = y (back sub for x).

rooms.question: Order the steps to solve a 2×2 system using Cramer's rule:
items: Form A₁, A₂ by replacing columns with b
rooms.explanation: Cramer's rule: compute det(A) first (check invertibility), form the modified matrices, compute their determinants, then divide.

rooms.question: What is the main advantage of LU decomposition over directly solving Ax = b by row reduction?
answers: Efficient for multiple right-hand sides: factor once, solve many times
It always finds the unique solution
It is more numerically accurate for 2×2 systems
rooms.explanation: LU decomposition factors A once. Each new right-hand side b only requires O(n²) forward and back substitution, not a full O(n³) elimination.

boss.name: The Decomposer
boss.taunt: Every matrix can be torn apart. Let me show you what lies inside.

questions.question: LU decomposition requires pivoting when:
answers: A zero appears on the diagonal during elimination
questions.explanation: When a pivot (diagonal element) is zero, rows must be swapped (partial pivoting). This leads to PLU decomposition: PA = LU.

questions.question: Using Cramer's rule on a 3×3 system requires computing how many determinants?
questions.explanation: You need det(A) once, plus det(A₁), det(A₂), det(A₃) for the three unknowns — a total of 4 determinants.

questions.question: For an n×n system, Cramer's rule requires O(?) operations while Gaussian elimination requires O(?)
questions.explanation: Computing n+1 determinants naively is O(n·n!) — exponential. Gaussian elimination is O(n³). Cramer's rule is only practical for very small n.
